texto: Marriage is not comfortable and harmonious. Rather it is a place of individuation where a person rubs up against oneself and against the partner, bumps up against the person in love and in rejection, and in this fashion learns to know oneself, the world, good and evil, the heights and the depths.
explicacao: Citação de Adolf Guggenbühl-Craig sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Marriage is not comfortable and harmonious. Rather it is a place of individuation where a person …” articula sabedoria, love, marriage em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como casamento e marriage.

frase_original: Marriage is not comfortable and harmonious. Rather it is a place of individuation where a person rubs up against oneself and against the partner, bumps up against the person in love and in rejection, and in this fashion learns to know oneself, the world, good and evil, the heights and the depths.

texto: For those who are gifted for the soteriological pathway of marriage, it, like every such pathway, naturally offers not only trouble, work, and suffering but the deepest kind of existential satisfaction. Dante did not get to Paradiso without going through the Inferno. And so also there seldom exist "happy marriages".
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adolf Guggenbühl-Craig: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “For those who are gifted for the soteriological pathway of marriage, it, like every such pathway,…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, happy-marriage, marriage pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e trabalho.

texto: For us the question is, has the marriage to do with well-being or with salvation? Is it a soteriological institution or a welfare institution?Is marriage, this opus contra natura a path to individuation or a way to well-being?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Adolf Guggenbühl-Craig que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “For us the question is, has the marriage to do with well-being or with salvation? Is it a soterio…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, individuation, marriage, com foco em casamento e marriage.

texto: A marriage only works if one opens to exactly that which one would never ask for otherwise. Only through rubbing oneself sore and losing oneself is one able to learn about oneself, God, and the world. Like every soteriological pathway, that of marriage is hard and painful.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Adolf Guggenbühl-Craig: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “A marriage only works if one opens to exactly that which one would never ask for otherwise. Only …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, marriage, salvation pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e trabalho.

texto: The indigenous peoples of the great tourist spots seem to lose their souls. All cultural, religious, and political efforts and ideals are crippled since the culture is engaged only in luring ever more tourists. It is not the contact with an essentially foreign population that corrupts the inhabitants of the great foreign resorts. It is the contact with great masses of people who are seeking fir the moment only well-being and not salvation that weakens and devalues the indigenous population.
explicacao: Citação de Adolf Guggenbühl-Craig sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “The indigenous peoples of the great tourist spots seem to lose their souls. All cultural, religio…” articula tempo, marriage, salvation em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como casamento e salvation.
